growth_records.cite: Aoyagi, Luciano N. et al. Diversity and characterization of the ammonia-oxidizing bacteria responsible for nitrification in tea field soils. Scientific Reports (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-18596-5. [PMC12475048]
growth_records.snippet: At the optimal pH (8.0), the specific growth rate (µ) was 0.48 ± 0.0017 d−1 (Fig 3b). ... The K_m values for ammonium oxidation by TNE100 ... were 0.98 ... mM (NH4+ + NH3).
growth_records.notes: Chemolithoautotrophic ammonia-oxidizing bacterium (beta-AOB, Nitrosomonas sp., ANI clusters with N. eutropha C91). Reported specific growth rate mu=0.48 d-1 at optimal pH 8.0, growth quantified from slope of log-transformed nitrite production during exponential phase (days 1-4). CONVERSIONS: mu_per_h = 0.48 d-1 / 24 = 0.0200 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.48 d = 1.444 d = 34.66 h (paper also reports G=ln2/mu in Fig 3b). Substrate=ammonium, product=nitrite, but NO quantified mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion rate reported, hence uptake_rates/secretion_rates left empty. Ammonium-oxidation kinetics: half-saturation constant Km = 0.98 mM (NH4+ + NH3) from ammonium-dependent oxygen uptake (Suppl Fig S7). Base AOB mineral medium composition cited from reference 106, not stated in text; buffer 50 mM HEPES for seed, 20 mM HEPES (pH 7.0-8.0) or 20 mM MES (pH 5.0-7.0) for pH-effect experiments. Temperature 25 C, 110 rpm.
